name: cross-loop integration emits exactly one event per cross-loop reference rather than duplicate per consumer
command: mkdir -p .claude-tdd-pro/cross-loop && printf '{"rule_id":"no-eval","origin":"pr-corpus","consumers":["standards","compliance","rubric"]}\n' > ref.json && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/cross-loop/emit.sh" --reference ref.json --log .claude-tdd-pro/cross-loop/events.jsonl 2>err.txt && [ "$(wc -l < .claude-tdd-pro/cross-loop/events.jsonl)" = "1" ] && grep -q '"consumers":\["standards","compliance","rubric"\]' .claude-tdd-pro/cross-loop/events.jsonl && echo ok 1>&2
